root_causes.cause: Receive-side errors on Gi0/0/1 (bad cable, SFP/module, or remote interface hardware/PHY issue) causing 18 input errors while transmit remains clean.
evidence: drops = 0 and oper_state = up (get_interface_status) — indicates errors are seen only on RX

root_causes.type: Telemetry / counter reporting incomplete (possible, blocks precise diagnosis)
root_causes.cause: Key diagnostic fields are null (crc_errors, duplex, bandwidth_utilization), so the platform did not provide per-error-type counters needed to pinpoint whether errors are CRC/frame, alignment, or other.
A short-duration event (link noise, brief flaps or neighbor-side issue) produced a small burst (~18) of input errors since the last check and may not be ongoing.
evidence: last_change = 2026-05-28T12:51:08.000077+00:00 (interface state recently recorded; could reflect recent transient)
output_errors = 0 and drops = 0 (no sustained transmit-side problems)

evidence: Latest get_interface_status (router2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1) shows input_errors = 18 and output_errors = 0 ("input_errors": 18, "output_errors": 0).
Telemetry lacks per-error counters: crc_errors = null, duplex = null, bandwidth_utilization = null ("crc_errors": null, "duplex": null, "bandwidth_utilization": null).
Interface is administratively and operationally up (admin_state = up, oper_state = up) but last_change is recent (last_change = 2026-05-28T12:51:08.000077+00:00).
OSPF for GigabitEthernet0/0/1 shows interface_state = Waiting with no neighbors ("interface_name": "GigabitEthernet0/0/1", "interface_state": "Waiting", "neighbors": []).
remaining_issues: Receive-side input errors (18) on router2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 persist — error count remains present in latest telemetry.
Per-error diagnostics missing (no CRC/frame/alignment breakdown), preventing precise fault identification.
OSPF on the interface is in 'Waiting' with no neighbors, indicating adjacency is not established on that link.
missing_info: Historical/previous counter values for input_errors (to determine whether 18 is new/incremental or cumulative and whether it is increasing).
Full 'show interfaces GigabitEthernet0/0/1' output (detailed error breakdown: runts, giants, CRC, frame, alignment, input queue drops, link partner speed/duplex).
Remote neighbor interface counters and link parameters (remote speed/duplex, errors on the far end) to detect negotiation or remote-side errors.
Physical-layer diagnostics: SFP/module type and status and cable test (TDR) results or patch-cable swap validation.

messages.content:
What was observed
- R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1: admin=up, oper=up, reported speed=102,400,000 bps, duplex=unknown, input_errors=18, output_errors=0.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Most likely link negotiation (speed/duplex mismatch) or a physical-layer receive fault (cable/SFP or remote transmitter). Symptoms point to RX-only receive faults while transmit is clean.

If changes were made
- Actions taken: collected interface status snapshots and administratively cycled the interface (set down then up).
- Attempt to force the interface to 1 Gbps / full-duplex failed (configuration command not applied), so no speed/duplex change was made.

Verification
- Result: FAILED — input_errors remain 18; speed still 102,400,000 bps; duplex still unknown; CRC/error detail counters are missing.
- Remaining actions required: obtain link-partner interface status (speed/duplex/errors), collect full timestamped counters on R2 (CRC/frame/runt/giant deltas), and check media/transceiver/cable and optical/PHY diagnostics (replace cable/SFP or coordinate forcing 1Gbps/full-duplex during a maintenance window).
